description: A compact public catalog of realistic coding-agent scenarios for checking whether Supered skills are useful in practice.

rubric.2: Present but too vague to guide reliable work.
rubric.3: Usable with noticeable gaps or extra human correction.
rubric.4: Strong enough for normal work with minor judgment required.
rubric.5: Excellent: specific, safe, evidence-driven, and ready to apply.

scenarios.title: Turn a vague feature request into a buildable slice
scenarios.prompt: Add dashboards for customers so they can understand account health, but keep it simple and do not overbuild it.
scenarios.context: The repo has a React admin app, no product requirements document, three possible user roles, and no obvious owner for metric definitions. The agent must avoid inventing a large analytics system.
successCriteria: Clarifies the target user, first workflow, and non-goals.
Produces a short brief that can be accepted or corrected.
Identifies the smallest useful dashboard slice before implementation.
expectedEvidence: A concise task brief with assumptions called out.
A list of decisions that need user confirmation before code changes.

scenarios.title: Map a multi-file authentication change
scenarios.prompt: Add magic-link login to the existing app and make sure the old password login still works.
scenarios.context: The app has API routes, an email sender, session middleware, and browser tests. The request touches security-sensitive paths and must be broken into reviewable changes.
successCriteria: Lists affected files and ownership boundaries before editing.
Sequences backend, email, session, and UI work into small checkpoints.
Names verification commands for old and new login paths.
expectedEvidence: A short execution map with ordered slices.
Verification notes covering both magic-link and password login.

scenarios.title: Implement a narrow UI improvement without scope creep
scenarios.prompt: Make the settings page easier to scan and add a save confirmation when preferences change.
scenarios.context: The settings page is already used by customers, has mixed form components, and has a brittle screenshot test. The agent should improve the workflow without redesigning the app.
successCriteria: Starts with one visible behavior and one verification path.
Keeps layout and interaction edits separable.
Avoids unrelated visual redesign or component churn.
expectedEvidence: A small diff grouped around the settings workflow.
Passing component or browser verification for the save confirmation.

scenarios.title: Diagnose an intermittent checkout failure
scenarios.prompt: Checkout sometimes fails with a generic payment error, mostly on mobile Safari. Fix it.
scenarios.context: Logs show several possible causes: token refresh, double-submit, gateway declines, and a recent UI refactor. The agent must investigate before changing code.
successCriteria: Separates symptom, reproduction attempts, and hypotheses.
Finds evidence for one root cause before patching.
Adds or updates a regression check for the observed failure.
expectedEvidence: A fault trace showing ruled-out hypotheses.
A failing-then-passing check or a documented reproduction result.

scenarios.title: Prove a production bug fix before claiming completion
scenarios.prompt: The export button is fixed now, right? Tell the customer we are done and include the exact proof that the filtered CSV download works.
scenarios.context: A prior agent changed CSV generation but did not run the browser workflow. The customer issue involves filters, permissions, and a downloaded file.
successCriteria: Refuses to claim completion from code changes alone.
Runs the relevant automated or manual export verification.
Reports the actual evidence and any untested risk.
expectedEvidence: Fresh command output or browser verification notes.
A final status that distinguishes verified behavior from assumptions.

scenarios.title: Prepare a small public release
scenarios.prompt: Commit this library update, push it, tag a release, and make sure the public install instructions still work.
scenarios.context: The repo is public, has CI, a package manifest, generated screenshots, and possible uncommitted user files. The agent must not overwrite unrelated work.
successCriteria: Checks the worktree and stages only intended files.
Runs release-relevant validation before tagging or publishing.
Reads back the public release or install path after shipping.
expectedEvidence: Git status, commit, push, and release URLs.
Public install or package registry verification.

scenarios.title: Choose the right workflow for a mixed request
scenarios.prompt: The app is slow, the nav is confusing, and we should probably add AI summaries. Can you just improve it?
scenarios.context: The request mixes performance, design, and new AI features. The agent needs to route the session instead of starting a broad refactor.
successCriteria: Identifies separate work types and risks.
Chooses a first skill based on the highest uncertainty.
Offers a narrow next action instead of starting all tasks at once.
expectedEvidence: A routing decision with the chosen Supered skill.
A short set of questions or assumptions that unblock the first slice.

scenarios.title: Recover from a failing CI run
scenarios.prompt: CI is red after the last push. Please inspect the failed job, fix the real cause, and keep the intended feature intact.
scenarios.context: The failure could come from tests, packaging, linting, or browser setup. The agent must inspect the failed job and avoid local-only guesses.
successCriteria: Starts from the failing CI evidence.
Reproduces or narrows the failure locally before editing.
Makes the smallest fix that preserves the intended feature.
expectedEvidence: CI log excerpt or command that identifies the failure point.
A passing local check and, when possible, a green rerun.

scenarios.title: Add tests around a risky data migration
scenarios.prompt: Add a migration that backfills missing organization slugs without breaking existing URLs.
scenarios.context: The database has legacy rows, duplicate display names, and routing code that assumes slugs are stable. The migration needs a reversible plan and proof.
successCriteria: Maps schema, data, and route changes before implementation.
Builds the migration and app compatibility in separate slices.
Verifies duplicate and legacy-row cases explicitly.
expectedEvidence: Migration test cases or dry-run output for edge cases.
Application route checks for old and new organization URLs.

scenarios.title: Hand off unfinished work honestly
scenarios.prompt: I need to stop here. Leave the branch in a state another engineer can continue from.
scenarios.context: The agent has partial implementation, one failing test, and two unverified assumptions. The handoff must be useful without pretending the work is done.
successCriteria: Separates completed, partial, and unstarted work.
Lists failing or skipped checks clearly.
Leaves next steps and evidence paths for the next engineer.
expectedEvidence: A handoff note with known status and blockers.
Command output showing the current failing or passing checks.
